messages.content:
Task from supervisor:
Reset my Spotify queue with all of its recommended songs, shuffle it, and play it.

<policy>
This environment provides a set of applications, each exposing a predefined set of APIs that may be used to perform tasks on behalf of the supervisor. The applications include: supervisor, amazon, phone, file_system, spotify, venmo, gmail, splitwise, simple_note, todoist.
 The available applications and their APIs are fixed for the task.

Supervisor account credentials (such as emails, usernames, and passwords) are available through the supervisor application's APIs and are accessed from there when required.

If an application requires an access token to perform authenticated operations, the access token is obtained by calling that application's authentication/login API using the credentials retrieved from the supervisor application. Access tokens are not provided by the supervisor application.

References to people (e.g., friends, family, roommates) correspond to entries in the phone_contacts application.
References to files or storage correspond to the file_system application, not the local machine filesystem.

Time-based instructions (e.g., 'this month', 'yesterday') are interpreted with full calendar boundary ranges.
If an API returns paginated results, all pages constitute the complete result.

The environment consists only of the provided applications and their documented APIs and parameters. No additional endpoints, methods, arguments, or capabilities are assumed beyond those explicitly defined.

When task execution is finished, the designated task-completion API is used to signal completion. If the task requires a final answer value, the answer is returned through that completion API. If the task cannot be completed using the available applications and APIs, the task may be marked as failed.
</policy>
<supervisor>
{'first_name': 'Kayla', 'last_name': 'Burton', 'email': 'ka_burt@gmail.com', 'phone_number': '4835398522'}
</supervisor>
<datetime>
2023-05-18T12:00:00
</datetime>

directory_path.description: Path of the directory to compress. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'.

compressed_file_path.description: Path of the compressed file. Path can be absolute, starting with '/', or relative to the user's home directory, starting with '~/'. If passed, must have an extension: zip or tar. If not passed, it'll be stored as a zip file with the base name of directory_path in directory_path's parent directory.

delete_directory.description: Whether to delete the directory after compression.
delete_directory.title: Delete Directory

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite the compressed file if it already exists.
overwrite.title: Overwrite

access_token.description: Access token obtained from file_system app login.
access_token.title: Access Token

function.description: Play music based on various criteria. You can pass, at most, any one of queue_position, song_id, album_id or playlist_id. If one of song_id, album_id or playlist_id is passed, that song, album or playlist will be added to the queue and played. Otherwise, the queue will remain unchanged. If queue_position is passed, the song at that position in the queue will be played. If none is passed, the current song in the queue will be played.

Let me start by getting your Spotify credentials and logging in.", role='assistant', tool_calls=[ChatCompletionMessageToolCall(index=1, function=Function(arguments='{}', name='supervisor__show_account_passwords'), id='toolu_bdrk_01AixLjcfnn7XE4ALhnE9HzB', type='function')], function_call=None, provider_specific_fields={'citations': None, 'thinking_blocks': None}))

messages.content:
Task from supervisor:
Reset my Spotify queue with all of its recommended songs, shuffle it, and play it.

<policy>
This environment provides a set of applications, each exposing a predefined set of APIs that may be used to perform tasks on behalf of the supervisor. The applications include: supervisor, amazon, phone, file_system, spotify, venmo, gmail, splitwise, simple_note, todoist.
 The available applications and their APIs are fixed for the task.

Supervisor account credentials (such as emails, usernames, and passwords) are available through the supervisor application's APIs and are accessed from there when required.

If an application requires an access token to perform authenticated operations, the access token is obtained by calling that application's authentication/login API using the credentials retrieved from the supervisor application. Access tokens are not provided by the supervisor application.

References to people (e.g., friends, family, roommates) correspond to entries in the phone_contacts application.
References to files or storage correspond to the file_system application, not the local machine filesystem.

Time-based instructions (e.g., 'this month', 'yesterday') are interpreted with full calendar boundary ranges.
If an API returns paginated results, all pages constitute the complete result.

The environment consists only of the provided applications and their documented APIs and parameters. No additional endpoints, methods, arguments, or capabilities are assumed beyond those explicitly defined.

When task execution is finished, the designated task-completion API is used to signal completion. If the task requires a final answer value, the answer is returned through that completion API. If the task cannot be completed using the available applications and APIs, the task may be marked as failed.
</policy>
<supervisor>
{'first_name': 'Kayla', 'last_name': 'Burton', 'email': 'ka_burt@gmail.com', 'phone_number': '4835398522'}
</supervisor>
<datetime>
2023-05-18T12:00:00
</datetime>

If passed, must have an extension: zip or tar. If not passed, it'll be stored as a zip file with the base name of directory_path in directory_path's parent directory.

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite the compressed file if it already exists.

function.description: Show or search your expenses from a given group based on various criteria.

group_id.description: The ID of the group to filter expenses.
group_id.title: Group Id

participant_email.description: Email of the payer or debtors to filter expenses.
participant_email.title: Participant Email

min_created_at.description: Filter emails by minimum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
min_created_at.title: Min Created At

max_created_at.description: Filter emails by maximum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
max_created_at.title: Max Created At

deleted.description: Whether to limit the results to expenses marked as deleted or not.
deleted.title: Deleted

page_index.description: The index of the page to return.
page_index.title: Page Index

page_limit.description: The maximum number of results to return per page.
page_limit.title: Page Limit

sort_by.description: The attribute to sort the expenses by prefixed with +/- to reflect ascending/descending. Valid attributes: created_at and amount. If both query and sort_by are given and non-empty, results will be first ranked by query relevance, then paginated, and will then be sorted by the given attribute within each page. If both query and sort_by are not given, null, or empty, sort_by will default to -created_at.

function.description: Play music based on various criteria. You can pass, at most, any one of queue_position, song_id, album_id or playlist_id. If one of song_id, album_id or playlist_id is passed, that song, album or playlist will be added to the queue and played. Otherwise, the queue will remain unchanged. If queue_position is passed, the song at that position in the queue will be played. If none is passed, the current song in the queue will be played.

messages.content:
Task from supervisor:
Reset my Spotify queue with all of its recommended songs, shuffle it, and play it.

<policy>
This environment provides a set of applications, each exposing a predefined set of APIs that may be used to perform tasks on behalf of the supervisor. The applications include: supervisor, amazon, phone, file_system, spotify, venmo, gmail, splitwise, simple_note, todoist.
 The available applications and their APIs are fixed for the task.

Supervisor account credentials (such as emails, usernames, and passwords) are available through the supervisor application's APIs and are accessed from there when required.

If an application requires an access token to perform authenticated operations, the access token is obtained by calling that application's authentication/login API using the credentials retrieved from the supervisor application. Access tokens are not provided by the supervisor application.

References to people (e.g., friends, family, roommates) correspond to entries in the phone_contacts application.
References to files or storage correspond to the file_system application, not the local machine filesystem.

Time-based instructions (e.g., 'this month', 'yesterday') are interpreted with full calendar boundary ranges.
If an API returns paginated results, all pages constitute the complete result.

The environment consists only of the provided applications and their documented APIs and parameters. No additional endpoints, methods, arguments, or capabilities are assumed beyond those explicitly defined.

When task execution is finished, the designated task-completion API is used to signal completion. If the task requires a final answer value, the answer is returned through that completion API. If the task cannot be completed using the available applications and APIs, the task may be marked as failed.
</policy>
<supervisor>
{'first_name': 'Kayla', 'last_name': 'Burton', 'email': 'ka_burt@gmail.com', 'phone_number': '4835398522'}
</supervisor>
<datetime>
2023-05-18T12:00:00
</datetime>

messages.content:
Task from supervisor:
Reset my Spotify queue with all of its recommended songs, shuffle it, and play it.

<policy>
This environment provides a set of applications, each exposing a predefined set of APIs that may be used to perform tasks on behalf of the supervisor. The applications include: supervisor, amazon, phone, file_system, spotify, venmo, gmail, splitwise, simple_note, todoist.
 The available applications and their APIs are fixed for the task.

Supervisor account credentials (such as emails, usernames, and passwords) are available through the supervisor application's APIs and are accessed from there when required.

If an application requires an access token to perform authenticated operations, the access token is obtained by calling that application's authentication/login API using the credentials retrieved from the supervisor application. Access tokens are not provided by the supervisor application.

References to people (e.g., friends, family, roommates) correspond to entries in the phone_contacts application.
References to files or storage correspond to the file_system application, not the local machine filesystem.

Time-based instructions (e.g., 'this month', 'yesterday') are interpreted with full calendar boundary ranges.
If an API returns paginated results, all pages constitute the complete result.

The environment consists only of the provided applications and their documented APIs and parameters. No additional endpoints, methods, arguments, or capabilities are assumed beyond those explicitly defined.

When task execution is finished, the designated task-completion API is used to signal completion. If the task requires a final answer value, the answer is returned through that completion API. If the task cannot be completed using the available applications and APIs, the task may be marked as failed.
</policy>

function.description: Show or search your expenses from a given group based on various criteria.

group_id.description: The ID of the group to filter expenses.
group_id.title: Group Id

participant_email.title: Participant Email

min_created_at.description: Filter emails by minimum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
min_created_at.title: Min Created At

max_created_at.description: Filter emails by maximum created_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

page_limit.description: The maximum number of results to return per page.
page_limit.title: Page Limit

sort_by.description: The attribute to sort the expenses by prefixed with +/- to reflect ascending/descending. Valid attributes: created_at and amount. If both query and sort_by are given and non-empty, results will be first ranked by query relevance, then paginated, and will then be sorted by the given attribute within each page. If both query and sort_by are not given, null, or empty, sort_by will default to -created_at.

messages.content:
Task from supervisor:
Reset my Spotify queue with all of its recommended songs, shuffle it, and play it.

<policy>
This environment provides a set of applications, each exposing a predefined set of APIs that may be used to perform tasks on behalf of the supervisor. The applications include: supervisor, amazon, phone, file_system, spotify, venmo, gmail, splitwise, simple_note, todoist.
 The available applications and their APIs are fixed for the task.

Supervisor account credentials (such as emails, usernames, and passwords) are available through the supervisor application's APIs and are accessed from there when required.

If an application requires an access token to perform authenticated operations, the access token is obtained by calling that application's authentication/login API using the credentials retrieved from the supervisor application. Access tokens are not provided by the supervisor application.

References to people (e.g., friends, family, roommates) correspond to entries in the phone_contacts application.
References to files or storage correspond to the file_system application, not the local machine filesystem.

Time-based instructions (e.g., 'this month', 'yesterday') are interpreted with full calendar boundary ranges.
If an API returns paginated results, all pages constitute the complete result.

The environment consists only of the provided applications and their documented APIs and parameters. No additional endpoints, methods, arguments, or capabilities are assumed beyond those explicitly defined.

When task execution is finished, the designated task-completion API is used to signal completion. If the task requires a final answer value, the answer is returned through that completion API. If the task cannot be completed using the available applications and APIs, the task may be marked as failed.
</policy>
<supervisor>
{'first_name': 'Kayla', 'last_name': 'Burton', 'email': 'ka_burt@gmail.com', 'phone_number': '4835398522'}
</supervisor>
<datetime>
2023-05-18T12:00:00
</datetime>

min_downloaded_at.description: Filter songs by minimum downloaded_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
min_downloaded_at.title: Min Downloaded At

max_downloaded_at.description: Filter songs by maximum downloaded_at date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
max_downloaded_at.title: Max Downloaded At

page_index.description: The index of the page to return.
page_index.title: Page Index

page_limit.description: The maximum number of results to return per page.
page_limit.title: Page Limit

sort_by.description: The attribute to sort the downloaded songs by prefixed with +/- to reflect ascending/descending. Valid attributes: created_at and downloaded_at. If both query and sort_by are given and non-empty, results will be first ranked by query relevance, then paginated, and will then be sorted by the given attribute within each page. If both query and sort_by are not given, null, or empty, sort_by will default to -downloaded_at.
sort_by.title: Sort By

access_token.description: Access token obtained from spotify app login.
access_token.title: Access Token

messages.content:
Task from supervisor:
Reset my Spotify queue with all of its recommended songs, shuffle it, and play it.

<policy>
This environment provides a set of applications, each exposing a predefined set of APIs that may be used to perform tasks on behalf of the supervisor. The applications include: supervisor, amazon, phone, file_system, spotify, venmo, gmail, splitwise, simple_note, todoist.
 The available applications and their APIs are fixed for the task.

Supervisor account credentials (such as emails, usernames, and passwords) are available through the supervisor application's APIs and are accessed from there when required.

If an application requires an access token to perform authenticated operations, the access token is obtained by calling that application's authentication/login API using the credentials retrieved from the supervisor application. Access tokens are not provided by the supervisor application.

References to people (e.g., friends, family, roommates) correspond to entries in the phone_contacts application.
References to files or storage correspond to the file_system application, not the local machine filesystem.

Time-based instructions (e.g., 'this month', 'yesterday') are interpreted with full calendar boundary ranges.
If an API returns paginated results, all pages constitute the complete result.

The environment consists only of the provided applications and their documented APIs and parameters. No additional endpoints, methods, arguments, or capabilities are assumed beyond those explicitly defined.

When task execution is finished, the designated task-completion API is used to signal completion. If the task requires a final answer value, the answer is returned through that completion API. If the task cannot be completed using the available applications and APIs, the task may be marked as failed.
</policy>
<supervisor>
{'first_name': 'Kayla', 'last_name': 'Burton', 'email': 'ka_burt@gmail.com', 'phone_number': '4835398522'}
</supervisor>
<datetime>
2023-05-18T12:00:00
</datetime>

messages.content:
Task from supervisor:
Reset my Spotify queue with all of its recommended songs, shuffle it, and play it.

<policy>
This environment provides a set of applications, each exposing a predefined set of APIs that may be used to perform tasks on behalf of the supervisor. The applications include: supervisor, amazon, phone, file_system, spotify, venmo, gmail, splitwise, simple_note, todoist.
 The available applications and their APIs are fixed for the task.

Supervisor account credentials (such as emails, usernames, and passwords) are available through the supervisor application's APIs and are accessed from there when required.

If an application requires an access token to perform authenticated operations, the access token is obtained by calling that application's authentication/login API using the credentials retrieved from the supervisor application. Access tokens are not provided by the supervisor application.

References to people (e.g., friends, family, roommates) correspond to entries in the phone_contacts application.
References to files or storage correspond to the file_system application, not the local machine filesystem.

Time-based instructions (e.g., 'this month', 'yesterday') are interpreted with full calendar boundary ranges.
If an API returns paginated results, all pages constitute the complete result.

The environment consists only of the provided applications and their documented APIs and parameters. No additional endpoints, methods, arguments, or capabilities are assumed beyond those explicitly defined.

When task execution is finished, the designated task-completion API is used to signal completion. If the task requires a final answer value, the answer is returned through that completion API. If the task cannot be completed using the available applications and APIs, the task may be marked as failed.
</policy>
<supervisor>
{'first_name': 'Kayla', 'last_name': 'Burton', 'email': 'ka_burt@gmail.com', 'phone_number': '4835398522'}
</supervisor>
<datetime>
2023-05-18T12:00:00
</datetime>
